risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: Political and governance risks remain at High level, directly impacting the project's ability to maintain implementation momentum and institutional coordination across Afghan government agencies.
evidence_quote: Political and Governance High High High

risk_summary: Macroeconomic risk rating has deteriorated from Low at approval to High currently, indicating severe economic instability affecting project financing and implementation capacity.

Institutional capacity for implementation and sustainability has worsened from Substantial at approval to High risk currently, suggesting government implementing agencies (Ministry of Transport, Ministry of Public Works) are unable to effectively execute project activities.
evidence_quote: Institutional Capacity for Implementation and Sustainability Substantial High High

risk_summary: Stakeholder risk has escalated from Moderate at approval to High currently, indicating deteriorating relationships between government, development actors, and affected communities.
Undisbursed IDA grants remain suspended pending World Bank decision, indicating a critical breakdown in implementation conditions likely linked to governance and FCV concerns in Afghanistan.
evidence_quote: Undisbursed IDA grants under the project will remain suspended until the World Bank makes further decision.

risk_summary: Progress towards PDO achievement has downgraded from Moderately Unsatisfactory to Highly Unsatisfactory, with minimal physical progress (2% pavement completion, 0% fiber optic installation, 0% roads constructed) indicating implementation paralysis likely driven by FCV conditions.
evidence_quote: Progress towards achievement of PDO Moderately Unsatisfactory Highly Unsatisfactory
